name: Elsevier Journal Article
description: Elsevier journal article using the IMRaD-extended structure. Suits applied-sciences, engineering, and life-sciences journals.
default_journal_type: Elsevier journal (e.g. Expert Systems with Applications)

sections.title: Title Page
sections.purpose: Title, author block, affiliations, corresponding author, ORCIDs.
sections.formatting_notes: Per Elsevier guidance, place affiliations directly below the author list.

sections.title: Highlights
sections.purpose: Three to five bullet sentences summarising the core contributions (Elsevier-specific).
sections.formatting_notes: Bullet list, each bullet under 85 characters.

sections.title: Abstract
sections.purpose: Structured summary covering background, method, results, and conclusion.
sections.formatting_notes: Some Elsevier journals require a structured abstract — use the labels above if so.

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.purpose: Frame the problem, establish significance, and state the contributions.
sections.formatting_notes: Numeric section heading. Close with a 'The rest of this paper is organised as follows' paragraph.

sections.title: 2. Literature Review
sections.purpose: Detailed survey of prior work with thematic synthesis.

sections.title: 3. Materials and Methods
sections.purpose: Methods, materials, and procedure — reproducibility is the bar.
sections.formatting_notes: Use subsections 3.1, 3.2 etc. Number all equations.

sections.title: 4. Experimental Setup
sections.purpose: Define metrics, baselines, hardware, and evaluation protocol.

sections.title: 5. Results
sections.purpose: Report findings with tables, figures, and statistical tests.
sections.formatting_notes: Use Tables 1, 2 etc. with captions above.

sections.title: 6. Discussion
sections.purpose: Interpret results in the context of prior work and theory.
sections.formatting_notes: Anchor each paragraph to a specific finding.

sections.title: 7. Conclusions and Future Work
sections.purpose: Wrap up and propose next steps.
sections.formatting_notes: Tight prose, no new citations unless essential.

sections.title: Acknowledgements
sections.purpose: Funding and contributor credits per Elsevier policy.

sections.title: References
sections.purpose: All cited works in Elsevier (Harvard) name-year style.
sections.formatting_notes: Surname, Initials., Year. Title. Journal Name, Volume(Issue), pp-pp.
sections.citation_example: Smith, J., Johnson, A., 2023. A title. Expert Syst. Appl. 213, 119123.
